title: schema: document implicit output contract for MIMR / VALI / SAGA
notes:
Inferred from skill instructions (no prior run logs available). Output contracts as understood:

MIMR outputs:
- Phase 2: tree-format audit report (stdout) — rows: { id, name, type, parentName, tokenBindings, rawBoundVars/resolved, hasTokens, hasBoundVars }
- Phase 2: summary line — { total, withTS, withNV, conflicts, unbound }
- Phase 3: audit log table — { result, count } with per-entry: nodeId, rule, key, value, status (applied/nv-auto/component-value/skipped/error)
- No file output — all output is conversational/in-chat

VALI outputs:
- Phase 1 (Analyse): OPS plan — array of { nodeId, name, currentType, action, proposedName, layoutMode, direction }
- Phase 2+3 (Execute): Figma write via process.figma.js; no file output
- Confirmation gate before any write

SAGA outputs:
- {name}.html — semantic HTML mirroring layer hierarchy
- {name}.css — vanilla CSS with --fds-* custom properties
- {name}.module.css — CSS Modules variant
- CSS var naming: last segment of NV variable name prefixed --

All inferred. UNVERIFIED — needs validation against real run logs.
Action: run each skill once on a real frame and compare actual output to this shape. Update these notes with diffs.

title: arch: separate skill contracts from implementation (defer)
notes:
Rationale: skill files currently mix contract (what the skill does, inputs/outputs, success criteria) with implementation (phase names, exact tool calls, file paths, grep strategies). This makes the contract implicit and brittle when Figma MCP or bd CLI changes.

Proposed split per skill:
  contract.md  — inputs, outputs, success criteria, handoff schema (stable)
  {skill}.prompt.md — implementation details (volatile)

ODIN-9000 depends only on the contract, not the implementation. This separation would let odin branch on real outcomes without reading full skill files each time.

DEFER until: 5+ real pipeline runs have been completed and failure modes are concrete.
Prior to acting: review issue fds-designer-0ro (output contract schema) — the contract.md content comes directly from that work.
